reply: Honestly? It's the manual handoff between our project management tool and GitHub. Every time a sprint wraps or a critical issue gets resolved, someone's manually updating statuses across two systems—it's killing our velocity. I've spent weeks trying to get a reliable webhook setup that actually syncs in real time, but the API documentation is vague, the endpoint reliability is inconsistent, and I keep hitting these edge cases that require vendor support to debug. We should be able to fire an event from GitHub, have it automatically update project status, trigger a Terraform run if needed, and feed into our custom dashboards without human intervention—but instead I'm babysitting brittle integrations and watching people waste time context-switching.

Honestly? The fact that every time I share work with a client, it looks like it came from some generic platform instead of from *me*. I'll spend hours polishing a brand kit, and then I have to hand it off through this impersonal interface that screams "tool made by some startup," which totally undercuts the premium positioning I'm trying to maintain.

It's not just about vanity—it directly impacts how clients perceive my professionalism and whether they'll hire me again or recommend me. If I can't white-label the client experience, I'm basically working for the tool, not for myself.
